        newTable
      • newTable(name: string; primaryKey: string; columns: openArray[ColumnDef]; primaryKeyMode: PrimaryKeyMode = pkmSerial): DbTable

        recoverFromWal
      • recoverFromWal(s: Store)

      • row(pairs: openArray[(string, Value)]): RowData
        where
      • where(t: DbTable; column: string; value: Value): seq[(string, RowData)]
  • Iterators
        allRows
      • allRows(t: DbTable): (string, RowData)
        allRowsByPk
      • allRowsByPk(t: DbTable): (string, RowData)

This module implements a simple WAL-based embedded database for Nim. It provides a Store type that manages multiple tables, each with a defined schema and supports basic operations like creating tables, inserting rows, deleting rows, and querying rows.

The Store can be configured to use either in-memory storage (for testing or ephemeral data) or disk-based storage with optional write-ahead logging (WAL) for durability.

Boogie is using BTrees and HashTables for efficient data storage and retrieval. The WAL is designed to support efficient group commits and to allow for recovery of the store state in case of crashes.

Imports

../wal

Types

ColumnDef = object
  name*: string              ## Name of the column
  kind*: DataType            ## Data type of the column (int, float, bool, text, json)
  nullable*: bool            ## Whether the column can contain null values. If false, a value must be provided for this column on insert.
DataType = enum
  dtNull, dtInt, dtFloat, dtBool, dtText, dtJson
DbTable {.acyclic.} = ref object
  name*: string              ## Name of the table
  primaryKey*: string
  case
  of pkmSerial:
  else:
    nil
  columns*: seq[ColumnDef]   ## Schema definition of the table columns
Represents a table in the store, including its schema and data. 
PrimaryKeyMode = enum
  pkmManual, pkmSerial
RowData = OrderedTable[string, Value]
A mapping of column names to cell values representing a single row in a table 
RowIndex = SortedTable[RowRecord, string]
An ordered index of rows in a table, keyed by primary key value. This allows for efficient ordered iteration and range queries. 
StorageMode = enum
  smInMemory, smDisk
Storage mode determines how data is stored and accessed:
  • smInMemory keeps everything in RAM (fast but non-persistent)
  • smDisk mode writes data to disk (persistent but slower)
Store {.acyclic.} = ref object
Represents the main store, containing multiple tables and managing persistence and WAL. 
StoreError = object of CatchableError
Value = object
  case kind*: DataType
  of dtInt:
    i*: int64
  of dtFloat:
    f*: float64
  of dtBool:
    b*: bool
  of dtText:
    s*: string
  of dtJson:
    j*: string ## For JSON, we store the raw JSON string. This allows us to
               ## preserve formatting and avoid double-encoding issues.
  of dtNull:
    nil
Value representing a cell in a table row

Procs

proc `$`(v: Value): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Stringified representation of a Value 
proc `==`(a, b: RowRecord): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Equality comp for RowRecord. Used by the SortedTable to determine if two records are the same. We consider two records equal if their primary keys are equal, since the primary key is the unique identifier for a row.

This allows us to update a row by deleting the old record and inserting a new one with the same primary key.

proc `==`(a, b: Value): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Equality comparison for Value. This is used for validating updates and for equality indexes. 
proc checkpoint(s: Store) {....raises: [IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Force a snapshot checkpoint now. 
proc createIndex(t: DbTable; column: string) {....raises: [KeyError, StoreError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create an equality index on the specified column 
proc createTable(s: Store; t: DbTable) {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, StoreError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Create a new table in the store. This will write to the WAL and commit the transaction. 
proc deleteRow(s: Store; tableName: string; pk: string): bool {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, KeyError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Delete a row by primary key. Returns true if a row was deleted, false if not found 
proc deleteRow(t: DbTable; pk: string): bool {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc dropTable(s: Store; name: string) {.
    ...raises: [IOError, WalError, StoreError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Drop a table from the store. This will write to the WAL and commit the transaction. 
proc getRow(s: Store; tableName: string; pk: string): Option[RowData] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Fetch a single row by primary key. This will be fast if the table has an order index, but will fall back to a hash lookup if not. 
proc getRow(t: DbTable; pk: string): Option[RowData] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Fetch a single row by primary key. This will be fast if the table has an order index, but will fall back to a hash lookup if not 
proc getTable(s: Store; name: string): Option[DbTable] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get a table by name. Returns none if not found. 
proc hasTable(s: Store; name: string): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if the store has a table with the given name. 
proc insertRow(s: Store; tableName: string; data: RowData): string {.
    discardable, ...raises: [StoreError, KeyError, IOError, WalError, ValueError,
                          OSError, Exception], tags: [TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect,
    ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
proc insertRow(s: Store; tableName: string; pk: string; data: RowData) {....raises: [
    StoreError, KeyError, IOError, WalError, ValueError, OSError, Exception], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Insert a row into the specified table with the given primary key and data. This will write to the WAL and commit the transaction. The primary key can be empty for tables with serial PK mode, in which case it will be auto-generated 
proc insertRow(t: DbTable; data: RowData): string {.
    ...raises: [StoreError, KeyError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
direct table mutation: no WAL here (store-level proc logs) 
proc insertRow(t: DbTable; pk: string; data: RowData) {.
    ...raises: [StoreError, KeyError, ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc isEmpty(t: DbTable): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if the table is empty (has no rows). 
proc newBoolValue(v: bool): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newColumn(name: string; kind: DataType; nullable: bool): ColumnDef {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new ColumnDef with the specified name, data type, and nullability. 
proc newFloatValue(v: float64): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newInMemoryStore(): Store {....raises: [StoreError, IOError, WalError,
    OSError, Exception], tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect,
                                RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Create a new in-memory store (no persistence, no WAL) 
proc newIntValue(v: int64): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newJSONValue(v: JsonNode): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newNullValue(): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newStore(path: string; mode: StorageMode = smDisk; enableWal: bool = true;
              checkpointEveryOps: uint32 = 0'u32;
              walFlushEveryOps: uint32 = 1000'u32): Store {.
    ...raises: [StoreError, IOError, WalError, OSError, Exception],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Create a new Store instance. Use smInMemory for an in-memory store (no persistence) or smDisk for a disk-backed store.

walFlushEveryOps:

  • 1 => flush every op (old behavior / strongest durability)
  • 1000 => group commit (faster inserts)
  • 0 => flush only on checkpoint/close/recovery-end
proc newTable(name: string; primaryKey: string; columns: openArray[ColumnDef];
              primaryKeyMode: PrimaryKeyMode = pkmSerial): DbTable {.
    ...raises: [StoreError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new DbTable with the specified name, primary key, columns, and primary key mode. 
proc newTextValue(v: string): Value {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc recoverFromWal(s: Store) {....raises: [IOError, StoreError, KeyError,
    ValueError, WalError, OSError, JsonParsingError, Exception], tags: [
    ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect, WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
proc row(pairs: openArray[(string, Value)]): RowData {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Helper to create RowData from an open array of (column, value) pairs. 
proc where(t: DbTable; column: string; value: Value): seq[(string, RowData)] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Return all rows where the given column matches the specified value. This will be fast if the column is indexed, but will fall back to a full scan if not.

Iterators

iterator allRows(t: DbTable): (string, RowData) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Iterate over all rows in the table in primary key order. This will be fast if the order index is clean, but may be slower if the index needs to be rebuilt. 
iterator allRowsByPk(t: DbTable): (string, RowData) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Iterate over all rows in the table using the hash index (unsorted).

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-05-06 15:56:00 UTC
